Football.title: Puma reveals 22 stunning World Cup kits including Cristiano Ronaldo’s final shirt and instant classic for England rivals
Football.content:
PUMA have dropped their stunning World Cup kits – including Cristiano Ronaldo’s costumes for his closing performance on the biggest stage of all.

The 2026 tournament in North America is almost certainly the 41-year-old’s last appearance at a World Cup.





Like his great rival Lionel Messi, the Portugal superstar made his debut in 2006 and has competed at each edition since.

But unlike Messi, Ronaldo is yet to get his hands on the greatest prize of all.

Now, though, CR7 can picture what glory at the MetLife Stadium on July 19 could look like – after Puma revealed the shirts.

The German sportswear giant launched the home and away jerseys for the ten teams who have already booked their place at the World Cup – plus one European nation hoping to get through the play-offs.

And there are some real gems among them – not least an instant classic that England will get a first-hand look at.

All the kits, available now, tap into the “culture, character and spirit of the nation” and its identity.

SunSport runs you through the 20 Puma kits – ten home and ten away – for countries competing at the World Cup – plus the Euro hopefuls desperate to join them.

Buy Puma World Cup kits

AUSTRIA

Kicking off with Austria and it is a really solid start. The home kit screams dependable with the red main, black sleeves and neat colour with white touches.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The away kit, meanwhile, is the opposite: a super bold alpine-inspired light blue number with what appears to be tie-dye pink fluid flashes and gold lines.

CZECH REPUBLIC

The Czech Republic have not booked their place at the World Cup and will need to get past the Republic of Ireland then either Denmark or North Macedonia in the Euro play-offs to qualify.

And who could begrudge backing the Czechs to get the job done and showcase these kits?



It is another case of a neat home red shirt – this time complete with white trim and a nice button-up design – and a far more out-there away shirt, white with a black “spontaneous” abstract design and gold accents.

EGYPT

Three times is a pattern, right? Another red home shirt, another pale away shirt.

Speaking of patterns, the Egypt home and away kits both feature triangular, geometric designs as a deliberate nod to their famous pyramids.



The red, black and gold for the home jersey is a winning formula while both are completed by a black round-neck collar for Mo Salah and Co to rock.

GHANA

Stop the count. We have our winners. Ghana stepped up after failing to qualify for Afcon by securing their place at the World Cup – and Puma have stepped up with two sensational shirts.

The home strip is a white base covered in vibrant geometric lines – in the national colours of red, yellow and green – with the iconic black star taking pride of place on the chest.



If the home kit is pure energy, the away kit is pure, unapologetically class. The unmissable gold offering has a Ghanaian textile Kente pattern all over with gorgeous red trim and again the black star.

It’s not hard to imagine Antoine Semenyo causing absolute chaos against England in Boston on June 23 in it…

Where to buy England's 2026 World Cup kits ENGLAND are dreaming of World Cup glory in North America this summer. And you can show your support for Thomas Tuchel’s men by wearing the new Nike kits. The white home jersey and red away strip were unveiled and went on sale in March. England home and away kits can be purchased online at the England Store. The AeroFIT kits cost £134.99 for adults, while the Stadium kit costs £89.99. A full kids’ kit – shirt, shorts, socks – costs £54.99. BUY ENGLAND KITS HERE

IVORY COAST

The nature of being one of the few countries to have an orange kit means Ivory Coast’s home jersey is always bright and brilliant.



The 2026 version is now different with more traditional textile designs giving a spotted effect and finished off with green accents while the away kit is white with green palm trees and golden suns.

MOROCCO

Moroccan football is synonymous with deep red and green – and it is no different this time around for their home kit, completed with a brilliant authentic pattern in the collar and sleeves trim – plus a central badge.

The away version may also be white but is totally unique with the pattern of Moroccan tiles and architecture running down the centre. Intricate and elegant.



NEW ZEALAND

Making a black kit interesting is no easy task – but Puma have done it for New Zealand with the textured design a tribute to the country’s beautiful natural landscapes.

The away is similar in many ways but “soft ice-blue”, with the swirling pattern possibly also referencing the seas that surround the islands.

Both kits feature “Aotearoa” on the back of the neck – the Maori name for New Zealand, commonly translated as “Land of the Long White Cloud”.



PARAGUAY

Strong Atletico Madrid vibes for Paraguay with a return of the classic red and white vertical stripes – although the lines are speckled, dabbed and unconventional rather than hard and binary – and completed with blue trim.

The away version is a dark piece reminiscent of the deep sea, with a black base and bluey-greeny elements and shadowy-comy effects.



PORTUGAL

Here is the one many of you have been waiting for – the Portugal kits Ronaldo and his team-mates will wear at the World Cup.

The red home shirt has a tonal, lighter red wavy pattern across the entire shirt – a nod to the “capturing movement and momentum” and the Atlantic waters that border the west coast.

The maritime theme continues with the punchy teal away kit complete with one full chevron on the front and the top area filled by more fluid designs all referencing the sea. Talk about going out in style, Cristiano.



SENEGAL

Senegal and their fans will hope to bring plenty of flair and fun, quality and creativity to the World Cup – and these two wonderful shirts will certainly help.

The home jersey has a white base with a whole plethora of traditional patterns and symbols, the centrepiece being the outline of a lion’s head for the Lions of Teranga.

It is a deep turquoise green for the away kit, completed by an energetic symmetrical central strip of geometry and yellow and red accents.



SWITZERLAND

Switzerland are known for their class and precision, so it is rather fitting that their red home shirt for the 2026 World Cup is a gorgeous red design with wavy pinstripes running from top to bottom.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

There is absolutely nothing understated about the away kit, though, with a white base covered with fluorescent green and turquoise abstract patterns, likely a nod to Switzerland’s rich flora and nature.

Unusually, both of the kits feature both the country’s national team badge and the national flag, the white cross inside a red square.

Football.title: John McGinn returns and Aston Villa start winning again — Dream Team bosses take note
Football.content:
THERE can be no doubt that John McGinn (£3.3m) is a vital cog in the Aston Villa machine.

The Villains’ woeful form has improved since the Scotland international returned from injury, thanks mainly to his decisive goals against Lille and West Ham.



McGinn lasted just 18 minutes against Everton in mid-January when he was forced off with a knee injury.



At the time, Unai Emery’s side had lost just one of their last 17 games in all competitions.

What followed was a miserable run of 11 games that featured five defeats and two draws.

Since McGinn’s comeback, Villa have won three in four.

It’s overly simplistic to suggest the 31-year-old’s absence was the only reason for the club’s downturn in form as he was one of several key midfielders unavailable but his importance to the team has been clearly demonstrated.

From a Dream Team perspective, McGinn is currently Villa’s most in-form player having banked 21 points across his last three outings.

His goal against the Hammers was beautifully controlled and a timely reminder of the quality he brings as well as the industry.

Dream Team managers who value form may have Villa’s No7 on their shortlist for Gameweeks 30 and 31.



Emery’s troops are one of just nine teams with a fixture after the international break, the first leg of their Europa League quarter-final against Bologna in Italy.

Then in Gameweek 31, they are among a select group of five sides with two fixtures in the diary, a trip to Nottingham Forest precedes the second leg against their Serie A opponents at Villa Park.

At the very least, McGinn is a viable option.

He appears in 1.4% of teams at the time of writing.



Gaffers keen on Villa’s upcoming fixtures but not in need of a differential might prefer Morgan Rogers (£5m) and/or Matty Cash (£3.5m) — the only two players in the squad to have collected 200+ points this season.

The former has scored ten goals and provided eight assists while the latter ranks joint-eighth among defenders.

It’s almost certainly foolish to speculate on which English teams will progress to the semi-finals of the European competitions at this stage but with Liverpool and Forest facing tricky ties against PSG and Porto respectively, it’s conceivable that Villa will be among the very last clubs active in Europe.

Arsenal are favourites to beat Sporting CP over two legs in the Champions League but Crystal Palace’s Conference League match-up with Fiorentina is harder to call.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

If McGinn and company do make it to the Europa League semi-finals they will have appealing double headers in Gameweeks 33 and 34.



But let’s not count our chickens just yet…

Football.title: Fifa forced to break own World Cup 2026 rule with stadium set to keep controversial feature
Football.content:
FIFA have been forced to break their own World Cup rule, months before the start of the tournament.

Sport’s most coveted competition arrives in the USA, Canada and Mexico this summer.





However, in order to protect the competition’s sponsors, Fifa demands venues across North America hide the branding on their stadiums.

That requirement has even extended to skyward-facing logos or lettering on stadium roofs.

Indeed, some of the 11 NFL venues that will play host to football this summer still don’t know how they will cover or conceal sections of their stadiums.

In this chaos, Atlanta’s Mercedes-Benz Stadium came to the unfortunate realisation they could not cover or remove the huge Mercedes star, which is made up of eight interlocking and movable panels each weighing 500 tons and stretching 220 feet long, on the roof without risk of serious damage.

The stadium’s vice-president of operations, Adam Fullerton, said in early 2025 he was confident the stadium would be able to cover all interior signage and build a grass pitch.

But he admitted covering the roof signage would keep him up at night.

As a result, Fifa has agreed to let the stadium roof remain as it is following 18 months of discussion, according to The Athletic.

A Fifa spokesperson told the outlet: “FIFA is working closely with stadium authorities and host cities to implement (brand protection) requirements in a manner consistent with previous editions of the tournament, while taking into account the unique infrastructure and operational considerations at each venue.”



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

The spokesperson added they would not comment on “specific arrangements relating to individual stadiums”.

SunSport has approached Fifa for comment.

At last summer’s Club World Cup, stadium hosts did not experience the same problem.

That is because those contracts were under standard rental agreements.

But for the Fifa World Cup, hosts signed a near 100-page contract.



Clause 6.4.ii of those contracts says hosts agree to, “the requirement that there shall be no advertising, marketing, promotion, merchandising, licensing, signage or other commercial identification of any kind on any stands, scoreboards, seats, seatbacks, time clocks, staff uniforms, Accreditation passes, fences or elsewhere inside, surrounding, or in the airspace above and around the Stadium other than that which is installed by, or at the direction of, Fifa or which is approved in writing by Fifa.”

The summer tournament is the biggest ever, with 48 teams spread across 16 cities.

All the action starts on June 11 and will end on July 19.

Football.title: Glamorous DAZN presenter Giusy Meloni shows off her keepy-up skills on touchline as fans call her ‘perfection’
Football.content:
GIUSY MELONI sent her fans wild by showing off her skills as she prepared to host coverage of a huge Serie A showdown.

The DAZN presenter, 26, was at the Stadio Olimpico for Roma’s clash with relegation-threatened Lecce.





And Giusy shared a video on Instagram where she got involved in some keepy-ups on the touchline.

The glamorous Italian stunned in a low-cut dress and sported a pair of black high heels.



Giusy’s fans raced straight into the comments section when she uploaded the footage and stunning snaps on social media.

One said: “If perfection exists, you are the embodiment of it. Beautiful!!!”

Another added: “Wonderful.”

A third chimed in: “The most beautiful in the world.”



A fourth wrote: “I’ll give football this, they have the undisputed BEST sideline reporters.”

A fifth commented: “Congratulations on your beauty.”

Giusy headed into the Serie A season by sharing bikini-clad summer snaps with her followers.

And she has continued travelling across Italy to cover some of the division’s biggest clashes.



The AC Milan fanatic has even been converting Inter fans to support their arch-rivals thanks to her stunning looks.

Roma grabbed a narrow win over Lecce as Guisy watched from the sidelines.

Robinio Vaz notched what proved to be the winner just before the hour mark – boosting the hosts’ Champions League qualification hopes.

Football.title: ‘I dread to think’ – Paul Merson gives Arsenal no hope of beating Champions League side after Man City ‘destroyed them’
Football.content:
BARCELONA could tear Arsenal apart in the Champions League.

That is the view of Gunners icon Paul Merson, who is now worried for the end of season run ahead at the Emirates.





Arsenal were beaten 2-0 by Manchester City at Wembley in the Carabao Cup final, surrendering after a strong opening 30 minutes.

And Merson believes that it opened up cracks which other sides could take advantage of to potentially ruin a largely brilliant season for Mikel Arteta‘s side.

He wrote for Sky Sports: “Watching that game and that second half, I don’t see Arsenal winning anything else but the Premier League.

“Man City are still in the FA Cup. Watching that game, you’d be shocked if Arsenal went back to Wembley and beat them.

“And then in the Champions League, I look at Barcelona – who Arsenal are on course to play in the semi-finals – and Arsenal haven’t got the pace to trouble Barcelona’s high line.

“The forward players aren’t electric – they’re not like Anthony Elanga or Anthony Gordon who caused Barcelona problems in the last 16.

“I dread to think what Barcelona could do to Arsenal.”

Hansi Flick‘s men are four points clear in their own title fight after a 15th straight home LaLiga win in a row on Sunday.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

They just put seven past Newcastle at the Nou Camp in the UCL and have one of the most dangerous frontlines around.

With Arsenal still competing to complete a potential treble plenty is at stake over the next two months.

And Merson can still see Arteta lifting a trophy in May despite missing out on a first piece of silverware since 2020 in this final.



He said: “Man City turned up in the second half of the Carabao Cup final and absolutely gave Arsenal a footballing lesson at the highest level.

“For 25 minutes of the second half, no one at Arsenal wanted the ball.

“They were booting the ball away, just kicking it back to City. And City destroyed them, absolutely destroyed them.

“It’s a sore one for Arsenal. It’s a confidence-sapping defeat because you come in the dressing room after that and think: ‘Oh my God, we just got destroyed in the second half.’

“Will it impact the Premier League? Arsenal are too good for the other teams they have to play against in the run-in, so they will beat those teams.”



Arsenal first have to get past Sporting, who came from 3-0 down to beat Bodo/Glimt in the last-16, in the quarters to set up that blockbuster tie.

Barca, meanwhile, are on the same side of the draw as Arsenal, and will face Atletico over two legs.

Diego Simeone‘s team are also Barca’s next league opponents.

It means three of the next four games for Flick will be against the same opposition.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Barca played Madrid twice in the Copa del Rey semi-finals earlier this year as well, losing the first leg 4-0 away before mounting a comeback at home, winning 3-0 but going out.



The Catalan giants are the third favourites for the competition behind Arsenal and Bayern Munich.

Football.title: ‘In five years I gave my all’ – Arsenal chief releases emotional statement after leaving club
Football.content:
ARSENAL technical director James Ellis has confirmed his departure from the club after five years at the Emirates.

Ellis joined the Gunners in 2021 as a scout for the first team, but rose through the ranks during his time in North London.





He was appointed technical director last summer as part of a restructure that involved Andrea Berta becoming sporting director.

But nine months into the role, Ellis has left Arsenal and confirmed the news via a statement on LinkedIn.

Ellis said: “Time to take the next step…

“Sport is a wonderful sector to work in, I am very very lucky to have worked in the industry for 25 plus years.



“My journey in Sport, from participation, rejection, education, coaching, scouting and senior leadership has made me who I am, and I owe this exposure to so many people along the way.

“I have been fortunate enough to work and contribute to some outstanding Sporting organisations, in the UK and abroad in High Performance and the Football industry (non league through to Elite level Premier League Clubs), which I hope in a small way I have left in a better position than when I started.

“I recently departed Arsenal Football Club, where in nearly 5 years I gave my all in an attempt to help contribute to supporting the development of elite level club and its high performance environment.

“The most notable memory of my time there, was coming across a number of wonderful people who (from various backgrounds and various levels of expertise), saw the world of opportunity, responsibility, custodianship and contribution the same as I do.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“I would like to wish the Club the very best for the future.

“Thank you to everyone who has so far in my career, helped shape me, learn so much from and help me find a course that supports my desire to support others, fulfill their potential and support organisations achieve their ambitions.

“I look forward to the next opportunity to continue to do so.”

Football.title: Crystal Palace line up shock move for Bournemouth’s Andoni Iraola to replace Oliver Glasner as manager
Football.content:
BOURNEMOUTH manager Andoni Iraola is on Crystal Palace’s shortlist to replace Oliver Glasner.

Iraola, 43, is out of contract at the end of the season like Glasner, 51, and is expected to leave the south coast club.





SunSport understands that Cherries head coach Iraola is among the names being considered by the Eagles as Premier League sides prepare for a summer of managerial merry-go-round.

Austrian Glasner, who led the club to their first ever FA Cup triumph, has already made his decision to exit Selhurst Park over the summer public while Iraola is deemed likely to move as well.

Fulham‘s out-of-contract Marco Silva is another linked to high-profile jobs and his future is just as uncertain.

Iraola, who has impressed with his brand of intense and attacking football, delivered a ninth-place finish last season at the Vitality Stadium.

The Basque boss currently sits one place above Glasner in 13th in the table this term.

He has won 37 of 107 league matches in England over nearly three years, seeing his team score 158 times, conceding 161.

Spaniard Iraola has also dealt with the exits of Antoine Semenyo, Milos Kerkez, Dean Huijsen, and llya Zabarnyi to top European clubs.

He is only three points ahead of Palace and would fall below the FA Cup holders if they win their game in hand by any score.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

This news comes after rumours of a return to home club Athletic Club in Bilbao for Iraola.

The former defender spent over 15 years there, playing more than 500 games.

He has been touted as a potential alternative to Ernesto Valverde – who has confirmed he is stepping down in the summer.



When asked about the reports last week, he said: “No, it doesn’t have anything to do with me.

“Probably as a supporter because it is my club, but no, it doesn’t affect the situation.

“I’ve said it a lot of times, I’m very happy here.

“I have a great relationship with the club, and it’s true that we will have to take a decision according to this, but it’s a situation I have had most of my years as manager.

“It is not new to me.”



Both Iraola and Glasner have been mooted as potential options for Manchester United and Tottenham with both teams looking for permanent managers as well.

Bournemouth are currently on a run of five consecutive draws in the Prem after an entertaining 2-2 result against United on Friday.

Their form extends back to 10 unbeaten since losing to Newcastle on penalties in the FA Cup.

Palace, meanwhile, are still competing for the chance to lift another trophy this season in the Conference League.

Their campaign has shown signs of unravelling at points with Glasner coming out and slamming the board for not delivering more in the transfer market.



At times it had looked like he would not make it to the summer without leaving.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He has managed to stabilise form and move the team away from the relegation zone.

As well as Iraola, Palace have been linked with Gareth Southgate and Sean Dyche.

Football.title: How Pep Guardiola’s 4-2-4 wall stifled Mikel Arteta’s Arsenal and wrecked their quadruple dream
Football.content:
PEP GUARDIOLA masterminded a 2-0 win over former student Mikel Arteta as Manchester City claimed the League Cup.

Arsenal were hoping to tick off the first title of what would have been an unprecedented quadruple.





However, those hopes were dashed as Nico O’Reilly scored twice in the second half.

From there, City outfought, outran and outplayed the Premier League leaders.

But the key to the door of their Wembley success was in the set-up City used to disrupt the Gunners’ game plan.

In association with Midnite, SunSport tactics guru Dean Scoggins takes a look at how City’s press stifled Arsenal.



Carabao Kep

It is impossible to look at this final without acknowledging the first mistake made by either side, that being Kepa Arrizabalaga‘s shocking missed catch that gifted O’Reilly the opener.

The goalkeeper has a horror history in League Cup finals, which struck once again, but Guardiola also specifically targeted the former Chelsea star to kill Arsenal’s way of retaining effective possession.

City set up in a 4-2-4 out-of-possession shape with Erling Haaland, Rayan Cherki, Antoine Semenyo and Jeremy Doku across the front four, but did not press Kepa when he had the ball.

On one occasion, the goalkeeper’s reluctance to play out saw him on the ball for a ludicrous 37 seconds, and City would not have cared one bit about that.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

And when he was finally forced into playing long, the City defenders would be aggressive to win the first header, where six attackers were then in front of them to try and win the second ball.

It did not help Arsenal that Martin Zubimendi had a poor game and Declan Rice was unable to get on the ball, and it was more disappointing to see Arteta not attempt to change the game plan at half-time.



Life O’Reilly

If Carlsberg did weekends, it would be just like the weekend O’Reilly just had.

A Cup-winning brace for a lad who had come through the club’s academy and turned 21 the day before… it doesn’t get much better.

City targeted the Arsenal right side, with O’Reilly moving into the left No10 position and forcing Zubimendi to pick him up.

Then Cherki would wheel out wide, and City would whip crosses in that would go over the head of Gabriel Magalhaes, therefore removing Arsenal’s best defensive header of the ball.

The second O’Reilly goal was down to him running down the inside channel, beating Bukayo Saka in the process.



Even the first goal, Zubimendi should arguably be doing better to not be beaten by O’Reilly.

Ballon d’Or Rodri

The Cup final was a reminder of just how good Rodri is.

He was the real key man for the City shape alongside Bernardo Silva, who also had an excellent game.

Rodri ended up having over 100 touches in the final, which is incredible for any player.

Thanks to the front line of blocks, when the ball was played over, Rodri would often win the header with Silva then picking up the second balls.



Rodri was also key to mopping up any messes left from the defence behind him.

Tactics Exposed is Sponsored by Midnite Midnite is definitely not your grandad’s bookie. Always remember to gamble responsibly — 18+, T&Cs Apply, BeGambleAware.org

He didn’t do anything spectacular, but did everything right at the base of the City diamond, which we highlighted in our preview show.

Overall, it was an all-round perfect performance from City.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Hindsight is a wonderful thing, and you could argue that playing David Raya instead of Kepa would have resulted in City’s tactics not being as effective.

However, the international break will be a good time for Arsenal to reset as they enter the final weeks of the season.

Football.title: Marc Guehi's reaction to being denied Man City Carabao Cup final medal says it all
Football.content:
Marc Guehi's reaction to being denied Man City Carabao Cup final medal says it all

Manchester City beat Arsenal to win the League Cup last weekend, despite Marc Guehi not being able to feature for Pep Guardiola's side at Wembley

View 3 Images Marc Guehi did not get a Carabao Cup winners' medal at Wembley (Image: Sebastian Frej/MB Media, Getty Images)

Marc Guehi was full of pride for his Manchester City colleagues while watching them receive their Carabao Cup winners' medals. This was despite not being able to get one himself.

‌



Guehi, 25, was prevented from featuring at Wembley Stadium in the 2-0 victory over Arsenal. The January transfer arrival wasn't barred due to being cup-tied, despite having already appeared in the competition this season prior to his move from Crystal Palace shortly after Christmas.

‌



Had this been the reason, fellow January acquisition Antoine Semenyo would have likewise been prohibited from competing beneath the Wembley arch. Rather, the England international was ruled out of the final because he wasn't registered at the club ahead of the first leg of City's semi-final showdown with Newcastle United.

‌



Semenyo was eligible to participate because he was signed and registered prior to the encounter against the Magpies.

Consequently, Guehi was unable to take part in the final against Mikel Arteta's outfit. Based on his latest social media activity, the centre-back was gutted he couldn't contribute to City's ninth League Cup success.

Declan Rice made one comment before Carabao Cup final that could come back to haunt Arsenal READ MORE :

He shared a slow-motion clip of City players lifting the League Cup trophy aloft during their celebrations in the Wembley stands. Guehi also added the caption: "What a team and performance. So proud of the boys."

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

Prior to the final, Guardiola made his frustration regarding Guehi's exclusion from the semi-final and final matches in the competition abundantly clear. In his press conference, the Catalan said: "Hopefully we can convince the Carabao Cup (EFL) that Marc can play the final.

"I don't understand why he cannot play the final. Hopefully we make a letter. You buy a player for a lot of money and he is not able to play for a rule I don't understand. Hopefully they can change it. It is difficult to understand."

‌



3 View 3 Images Guehi uploaded this video to his Instagram page (Image: Instagram/marcguehi) 3 View 3 Images Marc Guehi joined Man City from Crystal Palace in January

Instead, Abdukodir Khusanov and Nathan Ake were entrusted to anchor City's defence. The Uzbekistani and Dutchman delivered a flawless performance against the Premier League leaders.

‌



City triumphed thanks to a spectacular brace from Nico O'Reilly, who stole the spotlight with his surging runs into the box from left-back. Following the match, a delighted Guardiola said: "For James [Trafford] it was the first final he played.

"[Abdukodir] Khusanov just a year here, Nico O'Reilly has impressed me in his first full season and Antoine [Semenyo] played his first final. There are many players who have had a first experience but that is good, that's what we need. That tells us okay we can do it.

"Still we are much better than last season but not the team we should be. That requires time. Hopefully next season and the end of this season we can do it."

Football.title: Man Utd handed Andoni Iraola blow as Premier League rival make first move
Football.content:
Man Utd handed Andoni Iraola blow as Premier League rival make first move

Andoni Iraola is one of the most in-demand managers heading into the summer, with Manchester United, Tottenham, Athletic Bilbao and Crystal Palace all interested in the Bournemouth head coach

View 2 Images Andoni Iraola is in high demand (Image: Catherine Ivill - AMA/Getty Images)

Manchester United will face competition from Crystal Palace if they try to hire Andoni Iraola this summer. Iraola's contract with Bournemouth is set to expire at the end of the June and the Spaniard is already attracting lots of interest.

‌



Iraola is yet to make a decision on whether he will stay with the Cherries beyond the end of the season. And his potential availability has fuelled speculation he could land a prestigious job elsewhere, with United, Tottenham, Palace and Athletic Bilbao among those interested in his services.

‌



United will appoint a long-term successor to Ruben Amorim this summer, with Michael Carrick the frontrunner, having won seven of his 10 matches in charge as interim boss. Palace need to replace the departing Oliver Glasner, Athletic Club need to find a successor to Ernesto Valverde, who recently announced he will leave the club at the end of the season, and Spurs are in turmoil with Igor Tudor likely to depart soon.

‌



Iraola has a brilliant reputation after leading Bournemouth to 12th and ninth in his first two campaigns. Despite losing several key players in the summer transfer window, he has the Cherries on course for another impressive finish, with the south coast outfit currently 13th and unbeaten in 11 games.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Tottenham ready to rip up manager plans after secret Roberto De Zerbi talks READ MORE :

Michael Carrick handed huge boost over landing Man Utd job as update on talks emerge READ MORE :

The Telegraph reports that his work with Bournemouth has attracted Palace, who have known since January that they need to replace Glasner in the summer. The 43-year-old is considered one of the best young coaches in the league and Palace will try to tempt him into another 'project', rather than jumping into a higher-pressure job further up the league.

‌



Iraola is likely to be of interest to Athletic Club, who need to replace Valverde in the summer. Iraola made more than 500 appearances for his home-town club and that emotional connection could trump other options, although former Borussia Dortmund coach Edin Terzic has been strongly linked with the role.

For their part, United's chiefs are yet to hold talks with any prospective new managers, partly due to Carrick's impact as interim boss. Director of football Jason Wilcox and chief executive Omar Berrada are content to move slowly and could therefore miss out on Iraola, who has played his cards close to his chest.

View 2 Images Oliver Glasner will leave Crystal Palace at the end of the season (Image: Sebastian Frej/Getty Images)

‌



HAVE YOUR SAY! Where should Andoni Iraola go? Comment below.

"Definitely [closer to a decision]. It is something we will decide sooner rather than later, but there is no news," Iraola said when he was asked about his future earlier this month. "When there is something to say, we will say it."

Pressed further, he added: "I have one thing clear, the club and me are very open. We talk a lot and very sincerely. We have open conversations, but obviously we cannot say here, outside, what we are telling inside. I think that's how we should work.

‌



"I have a great relationship with [technical director] Simon [Francis] and [president of football operations] Tiago [Pinto]. Everything is fine between us. We have things that we discuss, but they are internal conversations."

Football.title: Noa Lang health update after Liverpool horror injury as Galatasaray doctor makes statement
Football.content:
Noa Lang health update after Liverpool horror injury as Galatasaray doctor makes statement

Galatasaray forward Noa Lang underwent surgery on a serious finger injury after colliding with advertising boards during the Champions League match at Anfield

View 2 Images Noa Lang of Galatasaray underwent surgery after suffering a finger injury during the defeat to Liverpool (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

Galatasaray winger Noa Lang will escape having his finger amputated despite sustaining a severe injury against Liverpool.

‌



Lang, 26, was stretchered off after colliding into the advertising hoardings during the closing moments of his team's 4-0 Champions League defeat at Anfield last Wednesday evening.

‌



The forward required oxygen treatment before being carried from the pitch on a stretcher, and subsequently underwent surgery at Whiston Hospital prior to his return to Turkey.

‌



In a statement, Galatasaray club doctor Yener Ince explained: “I want to talk about the condition of our two players who were injured in the Champions League Round of 16 match against Liverpool.

“Our player Noa Lang has suffered a serious injury to the first finger of his right hand. I want to clarify that there is no complete amputation; however, the injury is significant. Because this area needed urgent repair, he underwent a successful operation in Liverpool as quickly as possible, without being brought to Istanbul.

PSG handed injury blow ahead of Liverpool Champions League tie as statement released READ MORE :

“Currently, there appear to be no problems, but the Galatasaray medical team is monitoring him. We are in close contact with the Dutch national team's medical staff, with whom we have had good communication for a long time.

‌



“Lang will not play in the first match for the Dutch national team, and after further assessments, if conditions allow, we plan for him to return to the field in the second match. We aim for Noa Lang to return to the pitch as soon as possible with a special training program.”

Galatasaray have lodged an official complaint with UEFA and are weighing up legal action for compensation following Lang's horrific injury.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



The Super Lig club's general secretary Eray Yazgan told Turkish news outlet HTSport last week: "After the match, we lodged a complaint with UEFA representatives regarding the matter. They conducted their investigation.

"UEFA will evaluate the issue. We are consulting with lawyers. We will submit our compensation claim to UEFA. We will request that our grievances regarding salaries be addressed.

"If our request is not met, a lawsuit may be filed. Our priority is the health of our players."

‌



View 2 Images Galatasaray have provided an update on Noa Lang's health (Image: Martin Rickett/PA Wire)

After the match, Liverpool and UEFA officials were seen examining the advertising hoarding where the incident took place.

Lang had come on as a half-time replacement for Victor Osimhen after the Nigeria international had injured his wrist in an earlier collision.

Article continues below

And in the same statement, Dr Ince revealed: “Another of our players, Victor Osimhen, underwent a successful operation today. His recovery will be monitored daily, and our medical team will do everything necessary to ensure his return to the pitch as soon as possible. Updates will be shared with the public by our club throughout the process.”

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp would have new Liverpool issue after opening door on managerial return
Football.content:
Jurgen Klopp would have new Liverpool issue after opening door on managerial return

Jurgen Klopp has opened the door to a future return to management, with the former Liverpool boss currently working for Red Bull as global head of soccer

View 2 Images Jurgen Klopp is closing in on two years out of management (Image: Alexander Hassenstein, Getty Images)

Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has spent nearly two years away from the dugout since departing Anfield but has suggested he has at least one more managerial position left in him. Reuniting his old backroom staff could prove challenging, however, as several members of his Reds coaching setup have secured fresh roles elsewhere in football.

‌



Klopp spent nearly nine years at Liverpool before leaving at the conclusion of the 2023/24 campaign, having announced his impending exit in January 2024. He took time away before taking over as global head of soccer with Red Bull, though rumours about a managerial comeback continue to circulate despite him reportedly being happy in his current role.

‌



When the German confirmed his Anfield exit, he emphasised he would never take charge of another Premier League side apart from Liverpool. A position abroad remains a possibility, however, with Germany and Real Madrid both mentioned as potential options.

‌



Speaking at an event in his native Germany, Klopp dismissed that Real Madrid speculation but still left the door ajar for another managerial appointment somewhere down the line. Nevertheless, he's in no hurry to walk away from Red Bull at present.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

PSG handed injury blow ahead of Liverpool Champions League tie as statement released READ MORE :

"And as for the future, I'm not quite finished as a coach yet," he said. "So who knows what might come up in the next few years. But there are absolutely no plans in that regard."

‌



He may need to work with a new coaching team, though, regardless of where he lands. Some members of his supporting cast at Liverpool have moved into managerial positions whilst others have taken up various coaching appointments elsewhere.

Klopp's former assistant Pep Lijnders was touted by certain sections of the media as a possible replacement for Klopp. Instead, he accepted the manager's position at Red Bull Salzburg prior to the 2024/25 campaign.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



It was a short-lived spell in Austria for the Dutchman, however, and he was dismissed midway through the season. He has subsequently joined Pep Guardiola's backroom team at Manchester City, with whom he secured the Carabao Cup this month.

Vitor Matos arrived at Liverpool as an elite development coach under Klopp in 2019 and later accompanied Lijnders to Salzburg. The 37-year-old then embarked on his managerial career with Maritimo in Portugal's second division.

He didn't last long, albeit for different reasons from Lijnders, leaving Maritimo when he was offered the manager's role at Championship outfit Swansea City after Alan Sheehan's dismissal. The Swans occupy 14th position in the standings with seven matches left, rendering both promotion and relegation improbable.

‌



View 2 Images Vitor Matos is finding his feet in management (Image: Athena Pictures, Getty Images)

John Achterberg and Claudio Taffarel both contributed their expertise in goalkeeping coach capacities and neither remains at Anfield. Achterberg, who was at Liverpool before Klopp's arrival and spent more than a decade at the club, accompanied former Reds captain Steven Gerrard to Al Ettifaq and stayed in Saudi Arabia following Gerrard's exit.

Taffarel held a dual position during his spell at Anfield, simultaneously working with the Brazil national side. He stepped away from the Liverpool post in the summer of 2025 and now solely works with Carlo Ancelotti's Selecao.

Article continues below

One individual Klopp could potentially persuade to join him in fresh pastures is Peter Krawietz. Krawietz served his compatriot well at Mainz and Borussia Dortmund as well as Liverpool and presently holds the position of head of football philosophy at Red Bull.

He temporarily returned to an assistant manager position last season, serving under caretaker boss Zsolt Low at RB Leipzig, but didn't remain once Ole Werner assumed the manager's role on a permanent basis. This all suggests that, if and when Klopp returns to management, he may be required to assemble a completely different backroom team around him.

Football.title: Nigel Farage fury as Championship club's fans up in arms after Reform MP's stadium visit
Football.content:
Nigel Farage fury as Championship club's fans up in arms after Reform MP's stadium visit

Reform Party leader Nigel Farage has faced fierce backlash after posing with an Ipswich Town shirt at Portman Road stadium ahead of a political rally, despite previously saying politics should stay out of football

View 3 Images Nigel Farage's visit to Portman Road has not gone down well with many (Image: X)

Nigel Farage has provoked fury after posing at Ipswich Town's Portman Road on Monday, clutching one of the club's shirts. The controversial Reform MP has previously argued that football and politics should stay apart, yet he seized the chance to visit the Tractor Boys' ground ahead of a political rally launching the party's local elections campaign.

‌



Farage is understood to have booked onto a stadium tour and has subsequently shared photographs of himself holding an Ipswich Town shirt. Publishing a series of images on X, the 61-year-old quipped: "I've never been too bad on the right wing."

‌



The political leader also shared a video and remarked: "Best of luck to Ipswich Town Football Club for the rest of the season." The footage featured Farage implying he was amongst the candidates for the Ipswich manager's position.

‌



England's 2026 World Cup kits This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more Various Prices England Official Store Buy Now on England Official Store Product Description England and Nike have launched the new home, away and goalkeeper kits to be worn at this summer's FIFA World Cup. You can get free delivery on all orders with the code: ENGFREEDEL

He said: "A big end to the season for Ipswich, they're in contention to go back to the Premier League and let's hope they do. But if they don't I think there's a chance, and I've had a chat with a few people, on the manager's job coming up next season and I'm definitely on the shortlist. Trouble is, there's only one job I'm really after."

One fan branded the photograph 'absolutely sickening', whilst another highlighted: "We used to chant Ipswich lives in racial harmony back in the 70s and 80s." Another supporter, writing on the TWTD fans' forum, said they were "disgusted and embarrassed" that Reform had been permitted to use the club's premises, whilst a second described it as "a slap in the face to all decent supporters, our players, staff and history as a fairly multicultural club," reports the Express.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Others, though, pointed out Farage had made the booking commercially and the club found itself in a challenging position. It was suggested the Championship outfit could either accept the reservation and upset some fans or cancel it and risk being dragged into the media spotlight by Reform complaining about cancel culture.

It is understood no formal invitation was extended by Ipswich to the Reform Party leader to stage a photo opportunity and Portman Road Events, which handles private stadium tours, is understood to have accepted a booking. However, the complete details of what was intended were not made apparent to the club until social media posts appeared on the party's official account - while Mirror Football have approached the club for comment.

‌



Monday also saw Farage forced to abandon a walkabout in Felixstowe. The Reform leader had planned to walk the seafront, but with demonstrators present and chanting, a spokesman confirmed the event was cancelled following security advice.

View 3 Images Nigel Farage had a tour of Portman Road (Image: X/Nigel Farage)

A Reform rally at Trinity Park Conference and Events Centre also attracted a number of protesters, but the event was oversubscribed. An additional room was needed, with around 300 people reportedly unable to fit into the main hall.

‌



A protest organised by Ipswich Stand Up To Racism saw anti-racist groups, community activists and trade unionists assemble outside, asserting locals wanted to give Reform a "resounding unwelcome" and demonstrate "Ipswich is a multicultural town that rejects Reform's anti-migrant racism."

Dean Ryan from Ipswich Stand Up To Racism stated: "Beyond their racist rhetoric around migrants, Farage and Reform want to make ordinary people pay for healthcare, driving the last nail in the coffin of the NHS."

View 3 Images Many Ipswich fans were left unhappy by Farage's visit (Image: Getty)

‌



Farage's appearance at Portman Road follows Reform's launch of their own football shirt. The party has created a top in Reform UK blue, featuring the party's logo and a Union Jack, with shirts autographed by Farage fetching a higher price.

In both cases, spectators have highlighted the irony after the MP had previously suggested football and politics should remain separate. Speaking in 2021, Farage expressed his desire for the two spheres not to be linked.

"Gareth Southgate is out of touch with England fans," he declared. "They have a right to boo when players take the knee for Marxist BLM. Let's keep politics out of football this summer."

Article continues below

Predictably, Farage was branded a hypocrite. "Thought you wanted politics out of sport," one comment read. Another commented: "This you?" and referenced a previous social media post by Farage. "'Politics has no place in football' - Nigel Farage (Unless I can grift from it)" another remarked.

Football.title: KSI mocks non-league club as star plays in Baller League again despite investigation
Football.content:
KSI mocks non-league club as star plays in Baller League again despite investigation

Sak Hassan, who's under contract with part-time National League club Wealdstone, appeared in Baller League for the second week running on Monday, turning out for KSI's Prime FC

View 2 Images KSI and Prime FC's players wore vests mocking Wealdstone (Image: Baller League)

A non-league footballer has appeared in Baller League for the second week running, despite being slammed by his club over his involvement. Wealdstone star Sak Hassan made his second appearance for KSI's team, Prime FC, on Monday after the National League side opened an investigation.

‌



The Stones later confirmed that Hassan, 25, is not part of new manager Gary Waddock's plans, opening the door to a transfer before the National League deadline on Thursday. Chairman Rory Fitzgerald commented: "Sak and his representatives are aware he's in breach of his playing contract by choosing to feature in the Baller League.

‌



"For the sake of absolute clarity, Wealdstone FC retains his official player registration and any decisions relating to the remainder of this season remain in the gift of the club's Board of Directors."

‌



After Hassan's debut for Prime FC, Wealdstone said: "We are aware that Sak Hassan, under contract with Wealdstone FC, appeared in this evening's Baller League. The club was not approached for permission for Sak to participate, nor were we made aware of his involvement in advance."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Brighton veteran Jason Steele had to cancel family holiday due to shock England call-up READ MORE :

Following Monday's 8-3 win against Rukkas FC, KSI and his players wore vests with those exact words printed on them, mocking Wealdstone. KSI, who's a stakeholder in sixth-tier club Dagenham and Redbridge, added fuel to the fire by resharing Baller League's post of him and the players posing with the vests on X.

‌



"Read it and weep," the influencer wrote as a the caption. Last week, KSI was accused of tapping Hassan up.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Speaking to social media star Chunkz, KSI explained: "You mentioned him (Hassan) to me, so I talked to him and I was like, 'Bro, I've got a big thing coming'. At this point I didn't mention Daggers. I was like, 'Bro, I've got big things'."

‌



Chunkz then interjected and asked: "Dagenham and Redbridge," to which KSI appeared to nod. Dagenham's new co-owner continued: "So, there's that. And I also said, 'Bro, think of the club'."

View 2 Images Sak Hassan also played in Baller League last year (Image: Sky Sports)

Hassan has made 32 appearances for Wealdstone this season, having joined them from Hashtag United last summer. The winger also has 13 international caps to his name with Somalia.

Article continues below

Hassan will be looking to earn another two caps after being called up for Somalia's double-header against Mauritius. The 25-year-old posted an image of him preparing to fly out to their training camp in Mozambique on Tuesday.

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp's next job: Setting record straight, Liverpool 'possible' and dream role
Football.content:
Jurgen Klopp's next job: Setting record straight, Liverpool 'possible' and dream role

Jurgen Klopp still has several options available after rubbishing claims linking him with a new job

View 3 Images Jurgen Klopp has denied contact with Real Madrid (Image: Alexander Hassenstein, Getty Images)

Jurgen Klopp has again been linked with a new managerial position recently. Klopp shocked the footballing world when he revealed in January 2024 that he would be leaving Liverpool at the end of the 2023/24 campaign.

‌



He ended a hugely successful tenure at Anfield with a Premier League title charge and a Carabao Cup triumph. After taking a brief break to recover from the relentless demands of football management, the 58-year-old took up a role as Red Bull's Head of Global Soccer in January 2025.

‌



Yet, German newspaper Bild claims it is an 'open secret' that the role doesn't entirely match Klopp's exceptional skills as a man-manager. Klopp responded this week by branding suggestions he would be leaving the Red Bull position as rubbish, stating the Austrian media outlet behind the story "hasn't got a clue" and calling them "idiots".

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

With rumours swirling, Mirror Football now examines everything Klopp and different sources have stated regarding the potential next chapter in his career, from a return to Liverpool to taking on his dream position.

Real Madrid rumours

Klopp was left furious by reports claiming he could become Real Madrid’s next manager amid rumours that he’d been in contact with Los Blancos bosses. He was at the Magenta TV World Cup team presentation in Munich whether he had received a call about replacing Alvaro Arbeloa this summer.

‌



The German was clearly annoyed by the rumours, responding with: "It's good that we're talking about this. When is a story a story? When someone takes a sheet of paper and writes something on it? Or when there's actually something to it?

"What does the situation have to be? That Real Madrid called me at some point and said: 'Florentino Perez on the phone! Jurgen, how are things?' Or is it enough if OE24 (an Austrian media outlet) – no idea whether that's AI or written by people – writes some rubbish? That annoys me. You need to show a bit of discipline there. It's all just nonsense."

He went on to say that Madrid have "not called me once, not a single time" in his life, before sarcastically adding: "I'll take over at Atletico Madrid as well, preferably at the same time. Sorry Madrid, you'll have to ring first."

‌



Liverpool return

View 3 Images Klopp may return to Liverpool one day (Image: Clive Brunskill/Getty Images)

In the same interview, Klopp didn't dismiss the possibility of returning to management. He explained: "For my age I'm quite advanced in life but as a coach I'm not completely finished. I haven't reached retirement age.

"Who knows what will happen in the coming years? But there's nothing planned."

‌



That implies he hasn't entirely ruled out a potential return to Liverpool in the future. Indeed, Klopp has previously made it clear that if he ever returns to the Premier League - widely regarded as the world's best division - he wouldn't want to manage any other team but Liverpool.

As speculation about Arne Slot's possible departure last year intensified, the German left the door open to a potential Liverpool return in some form. Speaking on the Diary of a CEO podcast, he said: "I said I will never coach a different team in England, so that means, if [I did return], then it's Liverpool."

When pushed on whether he might eventually return, he provided encouragement with three straightforward words: "Theoretically, it's possible."

‌



Dream role

View 3 Images Julian Nagelsmann has a contract with Germany until 2028 (Image: Gabor Baumgarten/Sports Press Photo/Getty Images)

Klopp rejected the chance to take charge of the German national team when approached in 2023, although that's not to say the proposition didn't appeal to him. Following a disappointing campaign, he opted to remain at Anfield for another year to oversee a squad rebuild before ultimately leaving at the end of the 2023/24 season.

By the time of his departure, the opportunity to manage Die Mannschaft had passed as Julian Nagelsmann had already replaced Hansi Flick. However, should the position become available again, Klopp would find it difficult to turn it down, having already taken his much-needed break from management.

‌



His agent, Marc Kosicke, hinted as much last month when he said: "Jurgen feels very connected to this country. The question doesn’t even arise at the moment.

"I don’t think he feels he has to do it (manage Germany) at least once. Rather, he feels a sense of obligation, that he couldn’t always say no to the job."

Nagelsmann’s contract with the German national team runs until 2028 but a disappointing World Cup always has the potential to bring forward managerial changes.

Football.title: Man Utd brush off £44m blackhole after double blow and financial announcement
Football.content:
Man Utd brush off £44m blackhole after double blow and financial announcement

Manchester United have seen a drop in commercial revenue this season, with the club's training kit sponsorship agreement with Tezos expiring at the end of June

View 2 Images Manchester United have addressed concerns about a drop in sponsorship revenue (Image: Chris Brunskill/Fantasista/Getty Images)

Manchester United maintain they remain "a very attractive proposition for sponsors and partners" despite mounting concerns over a fall in sponsorship income. United confirmed a decline in commercial revenue in their second-quarter financial results, released last month.

‌



United's training kit sponsorship deal with Tezos was valued at £24million per season, but it came to an end in June. This means United have gone through this season without a training kit sponsor for the first time since 2021/2022, when their arrangement with AON came to a close.

‌



Matheus Cunha was photographed in a Tezos-branded hoodie in unveiling shots taken when he joined from Wolves, but the agreement had already lapsed by the time Bryan Mbeumo arrived at the club.

‌



The club are prepared for a further setback, as their sleeve sponsorship arrangement with DXC Technology, believed to be worth £20m per season, is set to expire at the end of the current campaign, reports the Manchester Evening News.

For four seasons, DXC has featured on the club's home, away and third kits. The multi-year partnership has seen DXC deploy their expertise to enhance the way United connect with supporters across their digital platforms.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Ex-Man Utd star training with Premier League club after being free agent for two years READ MORE :

United sources maintain there is a robust pipeline of new sponsors poised to come on board, and a club representative reinforced that view at the latest fans' forum when questioned whether there was concern about the "slip" in United's financial standing and about securing new sponsors.

The spokesperson said: "We continue to see strong commercial performance – we are one of the world’s biggest clubs and are a very attractive proposition for sponsors and partners. This is reflected in the regular conversations we have with brands from around the world.

"This season our revenues have dipped due to fewer matches played at Old Trafford and our men’s team not playing in European competition – it’s important we stick together and support the team in finishing as high as possible in the Premier League."

‌



View 2 Images Ruben Amorim left Manchester United in January after 14 months (Image: Getty Images)

The fans' forum delegates enquired whether supporters would bear the cost for decisions taken by the club, such as failing to secure new sponsors or sacking Ruben Amorim and his coaching team.

United said: "We reluctantly made the decision to part ways with Ruben to give us the best chance of finishing as high as possible in the league. We have a clear objective to return Manchester United to the top of domestic and European football.

‌



"We want to keep investing in the team and improving our facilities so fans get the best possible experience.

"We also need to make sure the club stays financially sustainable, taking inflation and rising costs into account. This is why it’s important we continue to drive revenues."

Football.title: Duckett withdraws from IPL to focus on keeping England spot
Football.content:
Ben Duckett has pulled out of the Indian Premier League in a bid to keep hold of his England place following a dire winter.

The left-hander was widely touted as the world's best all-format opener not so long ago but a nosedive in form towards the end of last summer bled into the winter, where he made just one score north of fifty in 16 innings.

A top-score of 42 in the Ashes was compounded by him being unwittingly filmed and seemingly drunk during a mid-series break in Noosa, with the video posted online, while he was an unused squad member in England's recent T20 World Cup campaign in India and Sri Lanka.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England captain Ben Stokes pledged his support for the under-fire opener Ben Duckett, following some social media backlash

Following a long winter on the road and with scrutiny on his spot in the Test side, in particular, Duckett has informed Delhi Capitals of his decision to withdraw from the IPL, which starts on Saturday.

"I'm desperate to play all three formats for England for as long as I can," Duckett told the Daily Telegraph. "This is a decision I hope I will look back on in 10 years and see as a pivotal moment in my career, and the right decision. I will being doing everything I can to be ready for England."

Overseas players who make themselves unavailable after being purchased at auction without valid reasons, such as injury, run the risk of a ban, so Duckett's decision means he could be ineligible for the IPL again until 2029, as well as missing out on a £200,000 payday this year.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Watch prime Duckett hit this brilliant reverse pull shot for six...

Duckett, though, intends to return to Nottinghamshire and fix any underlying flaws in his batting alongside head coach Peter Moores with the view to playing in the County Championship ahead of England's first Test of the summer against New Zealand, starting on June 4.

The 31-year-old revealed he has already started working with England fitness coach Peter Sims to get himself in the best shape possible.

"I've learnt a lot in the last year," he said. "Going from how I was being spoken about during the India series last summer, to how I am spoken about now. That's a ridiculously big dip.

"Not playing in the World Cup and being on the sidelines in India after such a tough Ashes series was a struggle for me. But it's lit a massive fire in my belly moving forward. I don't know where I stand, or what is to come."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rob Key believes there isn't a drinking culture within the team, after England received criticism for their break to Noosa, between the second and third Ashes Test, following claims it was like a 'stag do'

Duckett also admitted his regret at what happened in Noosa, where there were reports of excessive drinking by a number of players. England have since slapped a midnight curfew on players.

"I am sorry for that incident," he added. "It was not professional and shouldn't have happened. There is no hiding away from it."

Watch England's home international summer live on Sky Sports, starting with a three-Test series against New Zealand from June 4. Not got Sky? Stream cricket contract-free on NOW.

Football.title: How will Littler react to Berlin boos? 'He's not a favourite in Germany!'
Football.content:
How will world No 1 Luke Littler react to the Berlin boos on Night Eight of the Premier League? Sky Sports' Mark Webster says "you can't be everyone's favourite".

The 19-year-old has previously threatened not to play events in Germany after being jeered by the crowd in a number of recent competitions.

He returned to the country at the World Cup of Darts last June, appearing alongside rival Luke Humphries, but the England dream team lost to Martin Schindler and Ricardo Pietreczko in the last 16.

Captivate Captivate , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies.
